steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The first sentence states Rogerson's 2015 calculation was more accurate than the 1920 survey's result. The next sentence explains why: the 1920 calculation used only cardboard and string. The blank must introduce an explanation for a result that was already stated — it signals "here's why that's unsurprising."

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Likewise,': Signals similarity or addition between two parallel ideas. The two calculations are being contrasted, not equated. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To that end,': Signals that the following action was done in order to achieve the preceding goal. No goal-directed action is described here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Of course,': Signals that the following fact is an unsurprising explanation — naturally the older method was less accurate given its crude materials. This fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Ultimately,': Signals a final conclusion or end result. The sentence explains a reason, not an ultimate outcome. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
